theme: The office has personality — now give every pixel a memory, a quirk, and a reason to be watched.

ideas.title: Worker Nameplate Glitch on Error
ideas.description: Each desk has an implied nameplate — when that worker enters error state, apply a CSS flicker animation to it (rapid opacity flicker, tiny horizontal jitter, red tint) like a broken fluorescent office sign. Two keyframes, one class applied when status === 'error'. WHY: right now the error screen SVG already goes red and flashing but the *desk identity* — the nameplate, the label — stays crisp and normal. A flickering nameplate makes the desk itself look like it's having a crisis, not just the monitor. It's the visual punchline you notice at 3am across the room.

ideas.title: Activity Log Entry Flash
ideas.description: When a new activity entry arrives and prepends to the log, it currently just silently appears. Give it a brief 400ms color-flash on mount: green for 'complete', red for 'error', blue for 'system', bright teal for 'hire' — then fades to normal. A single CSS animation class added on insert and removed after the duration. WHY: the activity log is the heartbeat of the dashboard but it whispers. A new error entry should flare red and demand a glance. A hire entry should briefly shine. The data already has `entry.type` — this is purely a visual upgrade that makes the log feel alive instead of archival.

ideas.title: Bloberto Quote Clipboard Button
ideas.description: When Bloberto's speech bubble is showing a particularly great line, users already screenshot it and paste it in Slack. Give them a '📋' micro-button that appears on hover of the speech bubble and copies the current quote to clipboard, then flashes a tiny '✓ copied' confirmation for 1.5s. WHY: the quotes are the most shareable thing in the product and we make people do OCR on a screenshot to get them. One button, a navigator.clipboard.writeText call, and a tiny state toggle. The personality escapes the browser. That's marketing we don't have to write.

ideas.title: End-of-Day Countdown in Footer
ideas.description: Add a subtle 'EOD in 2h 14m' line in the office footer, counting down to a configurable end-of-business time (default 18:00 in local timezone). After EOD, flip to 'After Hours 🌙' until the next morning. WHY: offices run on invisible clock-pressure. The Bloberto dashboard already nails vibe-weather — but there's no temporal tension. A quiet EOD countdown makes you feel like this is a real team working toward a real deadline. It also contextualises stuck workers: Carlos stuck at 5:47pm hits completely differently than Carlos stuck at 9am.

ideas.title: Idle Worker Desk Object
ideas.description: Workers who are idle show their character standing at a desk — but the desk is completely empty. When a worker transitions to idle, randomly assign them one of four SVG micro-props rendered above their desk: ☕ a steaming coffee cup, 🦆 a rubber duck, 🪴 a small plant, or 📚 a teetering stack of books. Reassign on each new idle transition. The prop fades in slowly (0.6s) and fades out when they start working again. WHY: idle desks feel abandoned right now. A rubber duck says 'Dave is thinking'. A coffee cup says 'Maya is on break, not fired'. Small objects make desks feel inhabited between tasks — the office reads as a place people live in, not a status board.

ideas.title: Stat Dispatch Flip Transition
ideas.description: The STAT_DISPATCHES map in App.jsx produces clever phrases like 'skeleton crew', 'code red', 'everybody scattered' for the stats panel. When the active/error/idle counts change and a new phrase replaces the old one, the transition is instant — one text just replaces another with zero ceremony. Add a split-flap (departures board) animation: the old phrase flips up and the new one flips down over ~300ms using a CSS 3D rotateX keyframe. WHY: the dispatch phrases are the wittiest copy in the dashboard but they're invisible when they change. The transition IS the punchline — 'smooth sailing' flipping to 'code red' should feel like a board game moment. It's a tiny animation on a tiny element and it will be the most screenshot thing we ever ship.

ideas.title: Team Momentum Score
ideas.description: Compute a live 'momentum score' (0–100) from a weighted formula: +15 per active worker, +20 per completed task this session, -25 per error, -10 per stuck worker, +5 per active worker above the team average progress. Display it as a bold score with a label tier: 0-20 'Stalled', 21-40 'Warming Up', 41-60 'In Motion', 61-80 'On a Roll', 81-100 'Unstoppable 🚀'. Render it in the dashboard header as a single glanceable metric. WHY: the dashboard shows five individual worker cards but no single number for 'how is the team doing right now?' A momentum score turns five data streams into one gut-check. It's the thing a manager glances at when they open the tab. And 'Unstoppable 🚀' gives the team something to brag about in standups.
